breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters claims 350GB data breach at European Commission
breaking_news.summary:
ShinyHunters claimed a massive breach of the European Commission's cloud infrastructure, leaking over 350GB of sensitive data including mail servers and internal communications. The European Commission confirmed a cyberattack on its Europa.eu cloud hosting on March 24, which was quickly contained but resulted in data exfiltration.

This incident compromises a major EU executive body, potentially exposing confidential documents and contracts to the public via a Tor leak site. The breach highlights vulnerabilities in cloud account security and the growing threat of hacktivist groups targeting government entities for data exposure.

breaking_news.title: Iran-linked Handala Hack Team breaches FBI Director's personal email
breaking_news.summary:
The Iran-linked Handala Hack Team successfully breached the personal Gmail account of FBI Director Kash Patel, leaking photos and documents to the internet. The FBI confirmed the incident, stating that the exposed material is historical in nature and does not involve any government or classified information.

Targeting a high-profile law enforcement official signals an escalation in state-sponsored cyber operations against US leadership figures. While no classified data was compromised, the breach undermines personal security for top officials and demonstrates the reach of Iranian cyber actors.

breaking_news.title: Citrix NetScaler CVE-2026-3055 under active reconnaissance by attackers
breaking_news.summary:
Attackers are actively probing a critical Citrix NetScaler vulnerability (CVE-2026-3055) that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to leak sensitive data via a memory overread issue. This flaw, rated CVSS 9.3, affects NetScaler ADC and Gateway appliances configured as a SAML IDP and requires immediate patching.

Citrix NetScaler is widely deployed in enterprise environments for secure access, making this vulnerability a significant risk for data leakage across major organizations. Active reconnaissance indicates that threat actors are already scanning for vulnerable instances to exploit the insufficient input validation flaw.

breaking_news.title: CISA adds critical F5 BIG-IP AMP RCE flaw to KEV catalog
breaking_news.summary:
CISA added a critical flaw in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager (CVE-2025-53521) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog after evidence of active exploitation emerged. The vulnerability allows specially crafted malicious traffic to trigger Remote Code Execution (RCE) when an access policy is enabled on a virtual server.

This reclassification from a Denial-of-Service issue to a critical RCE flaw underscores the severity of the threat to network infrastructure security. Organizations relying on F5 BIG-IP for access management must urgently apply patches to prevent unauthorized code execution and system compromise.

breaking_news.title: Langflow RCE vulnerability actively exploited within hours of disclosure
breaking_news.summary:
Attackers exploited a critical Remote Code Execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-33017) in the Langflow AI framework within hours of its disclosure, prompting CISA to flag it for urgent remediation. The flaw allows attackers to execute arbitrary code without authentication by supplying malicious flow data to a public build endpoint.

Langflow is a popular tool for building agentic AI workflows, meaning this vulnerability could compromise AI infrastructure and sensitive data across numerous organizations. The rapid weaponization of the flaw highlights the risks associated with open-source AI tools and the need for immediate security updates.

breaking_news.title: TeamPCP compromises Telnyx PyPI package with malware hidden in WAV files
breaking_news.summary:
The threat actor TeamPCP compromised the Telnyx Python package on PyPI, uploading malicious versions that deliver credential-stealing malware hidden inside a WAV audio file. This supply chain attack follows previous compromises of Trivy and KICS, demonstrating a sustained campaign against developer tooling.

Developers installing the malicious Telnyx package risk having their credentials and sensitive data exfiltrated without detection due to the obfuscated payload. This incident emphasizes the critical need for supply chain security monitoring and verification of dependencies in Python environments.

breaking_news.title: China-linked Red Menshen APT deploys stealthy BPFDoor implants in telecom networks
breaking_news.summary:
The China-linked Red Menshen APT group has been deploying stealthy BPFDoor implants within telecom networks to conduct long-term espionage against government targets. These implants act as "digital sleeper cells" embedded deep within critical infrastructure to monitor communications and maintain hidden access.

Compromising telecom networks threatens entire populations and national security by enabling the interception of sensitive communications and digital identities. The sophistication of these implants makes detection extremely difficult, posing a persistent threat to critical infrastructure in the Middle East and Asia.

breaking_news.title: CISA warns of critical unpatched PTC Windchill RCE vulnerability
breaking_news.summary:
CISA and BSI issued warnings about a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-4681) in PTC Windchill and FlexPLM software that allows Remote Code Execution via deserialization of untrusted data. With a CVSS score of 10.0 and no patch currently available, German police have physically warned organizations about the imminent risk of exploitation.

PTC Windchill is widely used for product lifecycle management in manufacturing and engineering, making this flaw a high-value target for attackers seeking to disrupt critical industrial operations. Organizations must implement immediate mitigations while awaiting vendor patches to prevent potential system compromise.

breaking_news.title: Apple issues urgent lock screen warnings for unpatched iPhones and iPads
breaking_news.summary:
Apple is sending urgent lock screen warnings to users of outdated iPhones and iPads, alerting them to active web-based exploits targeting older iOS versions. The notifications urge immediate software updates to protect devices from ongoing attacks that leverage unpatched vulnerabilities in the operating system.

This mass notification campaign indicates a widespread threat landscape where attackers are actively scanning for and exploiting vulnerable mobile devices. Users who delay updates face significant risks of device compromise, highlighting the importance of maintaining current security patches on consumer electronics.

breaking_news.title: Coruna iOS exploit kit linked to Operation Triangulation campaign
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers discovered the Coruna iOS exploit kit, which reuses updated kernel exploit code from the 2023 Operation Triangulation campaign to target Apple iPhones. The kit includes multiple exploit chains capable of compromising iOS versions 13.0 through 17.2.1, suggesting a link to sophisticated state-sponsored actors.

The evolution of the Triangulation framework indicates that advanced persistent threats are continuing to refine their mobile espionage capabilities against iOS devices. This development underscores the ongoing arms race between surveillance vendors and mobile security researchers in protecting user privacy.
